stories.headline: Iglesia ni Cristo concludes three-day rally at Manila's Liwasang Bonifacio
stories.summary: The Iglesia ni Cristo concluded a three-day rally in support of Senator Rodante Marcoleta on July 2, after moving the gathering from the EDSA People Power Monument in Quezon City to Liwasang Bonifacio in Manila once Quezon City revoked the group's permit extension. Manila officials said no permit was required because the site is a government-designated Freedom Park, and Manila Mayor Isko Moreno suspended city classes as a precaution; crowd estimates varied, with the city's disaster office citing about 8,600 attendees at midday and police citing about 1,700.
stories.context: Marcoleta faces a possible plunder complaint over a 75-million-peso campaign contribution he did not declare, which Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla has said will proceed to the Sandiganbayan.

stories.headline: Senator says online extremist network '764' may have influenced Tacloban school shooting
stories.summary: Senator Risa Hontiveros told a Senate hearing on July 1 that the shooting at a Tacloban high school that killed three people may have been influenced by "764," an online network the FBI has called a national security threat and that Canada has designated a terrorist group. Hontiveros said the group grooms vulnerable minors through gaming platforms such as Roblox, Minecraft and GoreBox, building a sense of belonging before pushing them toward self-harm or violence, and said she has referred the leads to the National Bureau of Investigation for verification.
stories.context: Two minors opened fire at San Jose National High School, killing three people and wounding several others; the case has since prompted separate Senate and law-enforcement inquiries into online extremist networks targeting children.

stories.headline: Marcos begins four-day Canada visit to advance trade, energy and defense ties
stories.summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. arrived in Vancouver on July 1 for a four-day official visit, the first by a Philippine head of state to Canada in 11 years, and met with Prime Minister Mark Carney on cooperation in trade, energy, defense and maritime affairs. Marcos said the talks would "lay the broad strokes of a Plan of Action" for bilateral relations, and officials are also working to advance a Philippines-Canada free trade agreement.
stories.context: The trip proceeded despite an Iglesia ni Cristo rally that disrupted Metro Manila traffic this week; Marcos also cited Canada's support for Philippine positions on the West Philippine Sea dispute under UNCLOS and the 2016 arbitral ruling.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano's effusive eruption continues into 179th day
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology recorded fresh lava discharge and minor strombolian activity from Mayon Volcano's summit crater on the evening of July 2, in three separate observations between 7:04 and 7:46 p.m., as the volcano's effusive eruption continued for a 179th consecutive day. PHIVOLCS said the eruption continues to generate lava flows, pyroclastic density currents and rockfalls, and kept Alert Level 3 in place, with entry into the 6-kilometer permanent danger zone prohibited.

stories.headline: Enhanced southwest monsoon suspends classes in Antique, PAGASA warns of more rain
stories.summary: PAGASA said an enhanced southwest monsoon, strengthened by Tropical Depression Henry, brought inclement weather that prompted San Jose de Buenavista, Antique to suspend in-person classes on July 2, shifting students to distance learning. The weather bureau issued thunderstorm advisories for nine areas, including Cebu, Leyte, Negros Occidental, Iloilo and Capiz, and forecast scattered rains and thunderstorms over the western sections of Southern Luzon, Visayas and Mindanao.

stories.headline: Peso seen regaining ground as MUFG cites lower oil prices, more BSP hikes
stories.summary: MUFG Bank said the Philippine peso, which strengthened to 61.318 per dollar in June from 61.587, should gradually regain footing in the coming months on lower oil prices, improved government spending and additional rate increases by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas. MUFG expects two more 25-basis-point BSP hikes this year, lifting the policy rate to 5.25 percent by year-end, before an easing cycle begins in 2027.
stories.watch_for: MUFG projected the peso would weaken to about 61.50 per dollar in the third quarter before strengthening toward 61 by year-end, a forecast it said hinges on oil prices and a possible more hawkish US Federal Reserve.

stories.headline: DA, DILG sign deal exempting farm storage facilities from property tax
stories.summary: The Department of Agriculture and the Department of the Interior and Local Government signed a joint memorandum circular implementing a property tax exemption for agricultural storage facilities and warehouses valued at up to 3 million pesos, under the Sagip Saka Act. Agriculture Secretary Francisco Tiu Laurel Jr. said the exemption, which could save qualified warehouse owners about 12,000 pesos a year, is meant to lower storage costs and reduce post-harvest losses for farmers, fisherfolk and cooperatives.

stories.headline: Alex Eala becomes first Filipino to reach Wimbledon third round in Open era
stories.summary: Alex Eala rallied from a set down to beat Australia's Maya Joint 3-6, 6-2, 6-0 in the second round of Wimbledon on July 2, becoming the first Philippine player, man or woman, to reach the tournament's third round in the Open era. The 21-year-old, seeded 29th, said she "had to dig deep" after a slow start against Joint, who had beaten her in a final last year.
stories.watch_for: Eala is scheduled to face defending champion Iga Swiatek in the third round on Saturday.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone barrage on Kyiv kills at least 22
stories.summary: Russia launched a large-scale missile and drone attack on Kyiv overnight into July 2, killing at least 22 people and injuring dozens, hours after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned of an impending "massive" strike. Ukrainian officials said Russia fired 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones; air defenses intercepted most of them, but roughly three dozen weapons still struck more than 30 locations, including a nine-story apartment building, an ambulance station and a publishing warehouse.
stories.context: Russia's defense ministry described the strikes as retaliation for Ukrainian attacks on its oil and energy infrastructure; both sides have continued to trade long-range strikes despite periodic diplomatic efforts to end the war.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 2,295 as rescues continue
stories.summary: Venezuelan officials said the death toll from the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes that struck June 24 has risen to 2,295, with more than 11,000 injured and roughly 50,000 people still unaccounted for. Rescuers from Venezuela and six other countries pulled a 43-year-old security guard alive from a collapsed seven-story building in Catia La Mar on July 2, eight days after the quakes struck.
stories.context: The United Nations has estimated the disaster caused about 6.7 billion dollars in damage, and the World Food Programme has appealed for 50 million dollars to feed 500,000 people for three months; the United States has pledged 300 million dollars in aid.

stories.headline: UN rights body to hold urgent session on Sudan's el-Obeid crisis
stories.summary: The UN Human Rights Council is set to hold an urgent debate on the deteriorating situation in and around el-Obeid, North Kordofan, after Germany, Ireland, the Netherlands, Norway and the United Kingdom requested the session, saying about 500,000 civilians risk large-scale atrocities amid drone strikes and fuel and water shortages. The requesting countries said they plan to submit a draft resolution for adoption after the debate.
stories.context: Sudan's four-year conflict between the army and the Rapid Support Forces has killed tens of thousands of people and displaced more than 12 million, in what the UN calls the world's worst displacement and hunger crisis.
